fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs, with Charles Bannerman scoring the first Test century.
In ODI cricket history, Sachin Tendulkar of India was the first male player to score a double century (200 not out), achieving this feat against South Africa in Gwalior on February 24, 2010.
The first Cricket World Cup was held in England in 1955 (officially called the Prudential Cup). West Indies won the inaugural tournament by defeating Australia in the final by 17 runs.
Jim Laker of England holds the record for the most wickets taken in a single Test match, taking 19 wickets for 90 runs (9/37 and 10/53) against Australia at Old Trafford in 1956.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo, Uruguay.
The fastest goal in FIFA World Cup history was scored by Hakan Sükür of Turkey, who found the back of the net in just 10.8 seconds against South Korea in the third-place match of the 2002 World Cup.
Pelé is the only football player in history to win three FIFA World Cups, lifting the trophy with Brazil in 1958 (Sweden), 1962 (Chile), and 1970 (Mexico).
The UEFA European Championship was first held in 1960. The Soviet Union won the inaugural tournament, defeating Yugoslavia 2-1 in extra time in the final played in Paris, France.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating powerhouse Indonesia 3-0 in the finals.
Badminton made its debut as an official medal sport at the Summer Olympic Games in Barcelona, Spain, in 1992, where Alan Budikusuma and Susi Susanti won the singles gold medals for Indonesia.
The Uber Cup is the premier international women's team badminton championship, first held in 1956-1957. China holds the record for the most titles won, with 15 championships as of recent history.

The longest match in tennis history was played at Wimbledon in 2010 between John Isner of the United States and Nicolas Mahut of France. It lasted 11 hours and 5 minutes over three days, ending 70-68 in the fifth set.
The first Wimbledon Championships were held in 1877 at the All England Croquet and Lawn Tennis Club. Spencer Gore won the inaugural Gentlemen's Singles title, defeating William Marshall.

Steffi Graf of Germany is the only tennis player (male or female) to achieve the Golden Slam—winning all four Grand Slam singles titles and the Olympic singles gold medal in a single calendar year, in 1988.
Roger Federer of Switzerland was the first male tennis player to win 20 Grand Slam singles titles. He achieved this milestone at the Australian Open in January 2018 by defeating Marin Čilić.
Basketball was invented in December 1891 by Canadian physical education instructor James Naismith in Springfield, Massachusetts, using peach baskets and a soccer ball.
Wilt Chamberlain of the Philadelphia Warriors holds the record for the most points scored in a single NBA game, scoring exactly 100 points against the New York Knicks on March 2, 1962.
The Boston Celtics hold the record for the most consecutive NBA championships, winning eight consecutive titles from 1959 to 1966 under legendary head coach Red Auerbach.

The United States men's Olympic basketball team, nicknamed the 'Dream Team' and featuring superstars like Michael Jordan, Magic Johnson, and Larry Bird, won the gold medal at the 1992 Barcelona Olympics.
